name: Chinese Steamed Buns
recipeIngredient: 3 cups all-purpose flour (reserve 1 tablespoon for hand mixing)
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast and sugar in lukewarm water; let stand foamy (10 minutes).
2. Mix flour with the dissolved yeast mixture until well combined.
3. Knead dough on a floured surface until smooth and elastic (10 minutes by hand).
4. Cover dough and let rise until doubled (about 3 hours). Punch down, wrap, chill for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
5. Combine hoisin sauce, chili sauce, rice wine, soy sauce, scallions, garlic, and ginger in a bowl; marinate mushrooms.
6. Clean mushroom caps and spoon marinade into them, allowing to marinate (a few hours).
7. Cook bell pepper, scallions, and garlic until soft (5-8 minutes). Add mushrooms with marinade, cook until liquid evaporates.
8. Combine water chestnuts and hoisin sauce; cook until most liquid has evaporated.
9. Roll dough into a log, cut into 16 pieces, roll each into a ball, flatten into circles, add filling, seal edges.
10. Cover buns; let rest for 15 minutes.
11. Line steamer rack with cheesecloth or wax paper; arrange buns at least 1 inch apart in the steamer.
12. Bring water to a rolling boil; steam buns until puffy and cooked through (15-20 minutes).
13. Prepare dipping sauce by combining soy sauce, reserved scallions, vinegar, garlic, chili oil, and cilantro.
14. Serve hot with dipping sauce or refrigerate for later use.
Note: This recipe makes 16 steamed buns.
